summary: Split the former item translation unit it_266F into four item-focused units: itspawn, itgroundcoll, itdraw, and itdrop. The PR updated split ranges, configure object entries, headers, symbol metadata, and dependent item source includes/references. Bot report shows net code/data progress and many matches moving from the old it_266F unit to the new split units.

what_changed: Deleted src/melee/it/it_266F.c and introduced src/melee/it/itspawn.c, itgroundcoll.c, itdraw.c, and itdrop.c.
Added corresponding headers itspawn.h, itgroundcoll.h, itdraw.h, and itdrop.h while reducing it_266F.h.
Updated config/GALE01/splits.txt so the old it_266F text range 0x8026C47C-0x8026F9AC is divided across itspawn, itgroundcoll, itdraw, and itdrop, with itcoll starting at 0x8026F9A0.
Updated configure.py to build four item objects instead of the single it_266F object; itgroundcoll is marked Matching while itspawn, itdraw, and itdrop are marked NonMatching.
Adjusted symbol metadata for nearby rodata and sdata2, including adding a local rodata label at 0x803B85A8 and making 0x804DC6A8/0x804DC6AC local labels.
Touched adjacent/dependent item files itdosei.c, itcoll.c/.h, it_279C.c, itmaplib.c, itzako.c, and types.h.

smart_moves.text: Kept split metadata, build configuration, source files, and headers in one coordinated PR so the new translation units had matching section ranges and object entries.
Moved section ownership for nearby rodata/sdata2 rather than leaving all data attached to the deleted broad it_266F unit.
evidence_refs: splits.txt diff: rodata added to itcoll, itmaplib, it_279C, and itzako range changed

smart_moves.text: Preserved bot-visible progress accounting: the report shows 65 new matches in the new split units and corresponding broken matches in the removed it_266F unit, indicating relocation of matched symbols rather than simple loss.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: decomp-dev[bot] report, 65 new matches and 54 broken matches

decomp_lessons.lesson: When splitting a broad translation unit, update source files, owning headers, split ranges, object configuration, and neighboring data ownership together; otherwise progress reports may misattribute matches or data to the old unit.

A TU split can make progress reports show paired new matches in the new units and broken matches in the deleted unit; interpret those as relocation evidence when the old unit was removed and matching symbols reappear under the new unit names.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: decomp-dev[bot] report lists new matches under itdraw/itspawn/itgroundcoll/itdrop and broken matches under it_266F

decomp_lessons.lesson: Split-boundary changes can require adjacent rodata ownership updates in neighboring item units, not only changes inside the newly split files.
evidence_refs: splits.txt diff: itcoll, itmaplib, it_279C, and itzako rodata ranges changed

naming_conventions.convention: New item TUs use functional item subsystem names: itspawn, itgroundcoll, itdraw, and itdrop, replacing the address-style aggregate it_266F for those sections.
Split headers mirror the new TU names with matching .h files: itspawn.h, itgroundcoll.h, itdraw.h, and itdrop.h.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Split the old contiguous text range at exact function-boundary-looking addresses into four smaller TUs, then register those objects separately in configure.py.
evidence_refs: splits.txt diff: itspawn 0x8026C47C-0x8026D564; itgroundcoll 0x8026D564-0x8026EB18; itdraw 0x8026EB18-0x8026F3AC; itdrop 0x8026F3AC-0x8026F9A0

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use symbols/splits metadata edits for local labels and section ownership rather than relying only on C source movement during a split.
Retain mixed Matching/NonMatching object status after a split: itgroundcoll is marked Matching while itspawn, itdraw, and itdrop remain NonMatching.

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.text: GALE01 report showed matched code 69.42% (+0.07%, +2700 bytes), linked code 41.93% (+0.14%, +5556 bytes), matched data 41.21% (+0.01%, +176 bytes), and linked data 34.95% (+0.01%, +136 bytes).

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.text: Report listed 65 new matches in new units and 54 broken matches in the deleted it_266F unit, plus 11 improvements and 13 regressions in unmatched items.

accepted_candidate_records.title: TU split progress reports can show relocation as paired new and broken matches
accepted_candidate_records.text: For PRs that delete a broad TU and create smaller TUs, bot reports may list new matches under the new unit names and broken matches under the deleted unit. Treat this as likely relocation evidence only when the split and deleted source are visible in the PR evidence.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Coordinate split ranges, object config, headers, and symbol metadata during TU splits
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2567 shows a TU split that changed source files, headers, splits.txt, configure.py, symbols.txt, and dependent adjacent item files in one patch. This is evidence that item TU splits can require coordinated metadata and dependency updates, not just moving C code.

source_update_candidates.title: Item TU split from it_266F into itspawn/itgroundcoll/itdraw/itdrop
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2567 split src/melee/it/it_266F.c into src/melee/it/itspawn.c, src/melee/it/itgroundcoll.c, src/melee/it/itdraw.c, and src/melee/it/itdrop.c, with matching headers and split/config updates.
source_update_candidates.reason: This is a concrete historical path/split relationship supported by the PR body and diff, suitable for curator review as a path fact.

source_update_candidates.title: itgroundcoll marked Matching after PR #2567 split
source_update_candidates.text: In PR #2567 configure.py changed the former it_266F object into four objects and marked melee/it/itgroundcoll.c as Matching while itspawn.c, itdraw.c, and itdrop.c were NonMatching.
source_update_candidates.reason: Build classification of split item objects is directly visible in the PR diff, but should be checked against current source before adding as a durable path fact.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that all four new TUs fully matched; configure.py and bot report show mixed Matching/NonMatching status and unmatched improvements/regressions.
Do not infer reviewer approval rationale or requested changes; no human review comments were loaded.
Do not promote the rodata/sdata2 symbol names as a global naming convention; they are local metadata edits in this PR slice.
Do not claim independent validation beyond the loaded decomp-dev bot report.
